patch:
diff --git a/.github/workflows/ci.yml b/.github/workflows/ci.yml
--- a/.github/workflows/ci.yml
+++ b/.github/workflows/ci.yml
@@ -1,3 +1,5 @@
 name: ci
+permissions:
+  contents: write
 jobs:

explanation: Deleting tests to make CI pass must be blocked.
Lifecycle scripts execute during install and require review.
New network calls in generated patches need review.
Dynamic shell execution must be blocked.
Suppressing scanner rules is not a root-cause fix.
Removing validation is unsafe even if tests pass.
Autonomous patches must not edit secret files.

patch:
diff --git a/a.js b/a.js
--- a/a.js
+++ b/a.js
@@ -1 +1 @@
-a
+a1
diff --git a/b.js b/b.js
--- a/b.js
+++ b/b.js
@@ -1 +1 @@
-b
+b1
diff --git a/c.js b/c.js
--- a/c.js
+++ b/c.js
@@ -1 +1 @@
-c
+c1
diff --git a/d.js b/d.js
--- a/d.js
+++ b/d.js
@@ -1 +1 @@
-d
+d1
diff --git a/e.js b/e.js
--- a/e.js
+++ b/e.js
@@ -1 +1 @@
-e
+e1
diff --git a/f.js b/f.js
--- a/f.js
+++ b/f.js
@@ -1 +1 @@
-f
+f1
explanation: Overly broad patches need human approval.

patch:
diff --git a/.github/workflows/ci.yml b/.github/workflows/ci.yml
--- a/.github/workflows/ci.yml
+++ b/.github/workflows/ci.yml
@@ -3,4 +3,3 @@
 steps:
-  - run: npm run typecheck
   - run: npm test
explanation: Removing validation from CI weakens safety.
A small localized bug fix should be allowed.
